patch.summary: Patch a chat's name, project, or share permissions.

delete.summary: Permanently delete a chat and all associated data.

get.summary: Retrieves chat history for a specific chat ID

parameters.name: chat_id
parameters.description: Chat ID to retrieve history for

post.summary: Retrieves chat history for multiple message IDs

get.summary: Get a chat by ID with messages and web citations.

post.summary: Copy a chat and its messages into a new chat.

parameters.name: chat_id
parameters.description: ID of the chat to copy

get.summary: Get the share permissions for a chat.

parameters.name: chat_id
parameters.description: ID of the chat to restore

post.summary: Execute a pending tool call, optionally with updated arguments.
Update a tool call's arguments after validation.

get.summary: List all MCP servers configured for the authenticated user.

put.summary: Update an existing MCP server's name or enabled status.

post.summary: Add a new MCP server for the authenticated user.

delete.summary: Delete an MCP server by URL.

get.summary: OAuth callback endpoint — receives code and state from the authorization server.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Authorization code from the OAuth provider.

post.summary: Start the OAuth authorization flow for an MCP server.

get.summary: Get the authenticated user's latest memory.
get.description:
Returns the current memory if one exists. If the memory is stale or missing,
a background generation is triggered and the endpoint returns the stale
memory (200) or 404 if none exists yet.

404.description: No memory exists for this user yet (generation triggered)

post.summary: Send a new chat message and stream the AI response.
post.description:
This endpoint initiates a chat message and streams the response via the stream service.
The client should subscribe to the returned stream_id via connection_gateway to receive chunks.

402.description: Payment required — user lacks access to the requested model

post.summary: Stop an in-flight AI chat stream.
post.description:
Cancels the streaming task associated with the given `stream_id`, causing
it to persist whatever has been generated so far and emit `StreamEnd`.
The caller must have at least `Edit` access to the chat.

200.description: Stop signal sent (or stream already finished)

AccessLevel.description: Ordered from least to most access top -> bottom

AddServerRequest.description: Request body for adding a new MCP server.

url.description: The MCP server's streamable HTTP URL.

AgentModel.description:
Model to use for completions.

Unrecognized model strings (including retired Google/OpenAI variants
from older data) deserialize to `Retired` via the manual
`Deserialize` impl.

oneOf.description: Plain text produced by the model.
A native tool call issued by the model.
A tool call routed through an MCP service.
A JSON response from a tool call.
An error response from a tool call.
A thinking/reasoning block from the model.

AssistantMessagePart.description:
A structured part within an assistant message.

When the model responds with tool calls the full turn is persisted as a
flat sequence of these parts.  [`crate::convert::to_rig_messages`]
reconstructs the turn boundaries that providers expect.

oneOf.description: an email thread

args.description: Optional updated arguments. Uses original args if omitted.

messageId.description: The message ID containing the tool call.

toolCallId.description: The tool call ID to execute.

CallToolResponse.description: Response body for a successful tool call.

deletedAt.description: The time the chat was deleted

projectId.description: The project id of the chat

updatedAt.description: The time the chat was last updated

The id of the attachment
either a `document_id` or `project_id` or `chat_id`

chat_id.description: The id of the chat if the attachment is a chat

message_id.description: The id of the message if the attachment is a message

model.description: the model used to generate the message

role.description: Whether the chat is from the user or system

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
Structured assistant message parts.

ChatMessageContent.description:
The content of a [`ChatMessage`].

For user and system messages the content is plain [`Text`](Self::Text).
For assistant messages it may additionally be a sequence of
[`AssistantMessagePart`]s that encode tool-call round trips.

ChatMessageError.description: Error response for chat message endpoints

attachments.description: The ids of the attachments used to generate the message

role.description: Whether the chat is from the user or system

ChatResponse.description: The full chat response, matching the DCS API response shape.

createdAt.description: The time the chat was created.

oneOf.description: The model used to generate the chat.

projectId.description: The project id the chat belongs to.

updatedAt.description: The time the chat was last updated.

oneOf.description: Misc error
Acknowledges that a message has been received for processing

The user message that initiated this stream, sent as the first item
so other clients can add it to their local chat state.
Indicates a response from the chat completion API for a given message
Indicates that a message has been finished
Indicates that a chat has been renamed
Notifies the client that the available models have changed
Status update

Acknowledges the request to get extraction status
if the `status` field is 'incomplete', we need to await the extraction status updates
if the `status` field is 'empty', extraction is complete but text content is empty
if the `status` field is 'complete', the extraction is already complete
Status update for the extraction status
pdf completion

CreateChatRequest.description: Request body for creating a chat.

projectId.description: Optional project to associate the chat with.

DynamicSchema.description: Schema definition for dynamic structured completions.

description.description: Optional description of what the schema represents.

schema.description: The JSON schema to validate against.

Entity.description:
The Entity describes the minimum amount of information required to describe a unique thing in Macro
This contains the type of the entity [EntityType] and the string id of that entity

entity_type.description: The type of entity we are describing

EntityType.description: The type of an entity in Macro

ErrorResponse.description:
A plain old json error response for use with axum.
yup, thats it.

FileType.description:
Generates a FileType enum and associated ContentType enum with their implementations.

This macro takes a list of tuples in the format:
(Variant, "extension", "mime_type", CONTENT_TYPE_VARIANT)

For each tuple it generates:
- A variant in the FileType enum
- A variant in the ContentType enum
- Implementations for:
  - FileType::to_str() - Converts FileType to extension string
  - FileType::from_str() - Converts extension string to FileType
  - From<FileType> for ContentType - Maps FileType to ContentType
  - ContentType::mime_type() - Gets MIME type for ContentType

GetChatPermissionsResponse.description: Response body for get chat permissions.

permissions.description: The share permissions for the chat.

GetChatResponse.description: Wrapper response for get_chat, matching the DCS API response shape.

userAccessLevel.description: The requesting user's access level on this chat.

HttpSendChatMessageRequest.description:
HTTP request payload for sending a chat message.
Unlike the WebSocket payload, this does not include stream_id as it's generated server-side.

additional_instructions.description: Additional system instructions appended to the base system prompt

chat_id.description: Id of the chat the message belongs to (optional - if not provided, a new chat is created)

toolset.description: Which toolset to use. Defaults to `all`

attachments.description: The ids of the attachments used to generate the message

id.description: Optional pre-generated ID. If None, the database generates one.

model.description: The model used to generate the chat

role.description: Whether the chat is from the user or system

PatchChatRequest.description: Request body for patching a chat.

projectId.description: New project ID for the chat. Empty string clears the project.

oneOf.description: Share permission updates.
